full_prompt:
Design a CFRP (IM7/8552) composite small-helicopter-style rotor blade using B-basis material allowables from MIL-HDBK-17 (CMH-17) Vol. 1 tabulations. The blade is a 2.0 m radius single-piece blade for a 200 kg-class tail-rotor demonstrator operating at 1200 rpm. MIL-HDBK-17 (CMH-17) provides Tensile and Compressive B-basis strength values for IM7/8552 UD tape and plain-weave composites, environmental knockdown factors for hot/wet operation (typically 0.80 for 82 C / wet relative to RTD), and damage-tolerance guidance (BVID 6 J impact, no reduction in ultimate strength after non-visible damage).

Geometry. Blade radius 2.0 m, chord 120 mm constant, thickness/chord ratio 12% => section height 14.4 mm. Airfoil-like symmetric section. Root pitch bearing attach: two 12 mm diameter Ti bushings through the spar, 50 mm apart. Spar: unidirectional IM7/8552 tape caps in the top and bottom of the airfoil, each cap 80 mm wide x 3 mm thick nominal (design variable 2-5 mm). Shear web: 4-ply +/-45 plain-weave 8552/AS4. Skin: 2-ply +/-45 plain-weave 8552/AS4. Root fitting: Ti lugs machined from 6Al-4V, 40 mm x 25 mm x 15 mm each; but root-fitting design is OUT of scope; only the composite blade is evaluated. Leading-edge erosion strip (nickel) 0.3 mm x 20 mm ignored structurally.

Material. IM7/8552 UD tape (CMH-17 B-basis, RTD): E_L = 165 GPa, E_T = 8.8 GPa, G_LT = 5.0 GPa, nu_LT = 0.3, rho = 1570 kg/m^3. Strength allowables B-basis RTD: Xt = 2280 MPa, Xc = 1440 MPa, Yt = 65 MPa, Yc = 170 MPa, S = 80 MPa. Hot/wet knockdown factor: 0.80 applied to all strengths for 82 C / wet service => Xt_HW = 1824 MPa, Xc_HW = 1152 MPa, Yt_HW = 52 MPa, Yc_HW = 136 MPa, S_HW = 64 MPa. 8552/AS4 plain weave B-basis RTD: tensile 700 MPa, compression 600 MPa, shear 100 MPa; hot/wet: 560 / 480 / 80.

Service envelope. Rotational speed 1200 rpm = 125.66 rad/s. Centrifugal force at blade tip (lumped 0.15 kg at r = 2.0 m): F_cf_tip = 0.15 * 125.66^2 * 2.0 = 4737 N; total distributed centrifugal for 0.8 kg blade lineal mass m/l = 0.4 kg/m gives axial tension at root = integral(m * omega^2 * r dr) from 0 to 2.0 = 0.4 * 125.66^2 * 2.0^2 / 2 = 12640 N at root. Flap bending moment at root under 1.5 g collective-pitch limit: M_flap = 1.5 * W_blade * 9.81 * r_cp with r_cp approx 1.3 m, giving M_flap_limit = 1.5 * 0.8 * 9.81 * 1.3 = 15.3 N*m per blade, but aerodynamic lift distribution amplifies this to approx 120 N*m limit root moment. Chord bending from lag: 30 N*m limit. Ultimate = 1.5 x limit.

Load cases. LC1 (steady flight, centrifugal + lift bending): axial 12,640 N root tension + 120 N*m flap bending, at RTD RTD allowables. LC2 (ultimate maneuver 1.5 x LC1): axial 18,960 N + 180 N*m. LC3 (hot/wet degraded allowables): LC1 state evaluated against hot/wet 0.80 knockdown allowables. LC4 (BVID damage tolerance): 6 J impact damage at the blade mid-span spar cap; ultimate strength must not drop below LC2 demand. Treat as a residual-strength check where the damaged allowable is 0.85 x Xt_B-basis RTD = 0.85 x 2280 = 1938 MPa. LC5 (fatigue): 10^7 cycles at combined centrifugal + 0.5 x bending reversal (typical rotor mission fatigue bound). S-N curve per CMH-17: 10^7 cycles at R = 0.1 for UD tape, allowable stress amplitude approx 0.4 x Xt_B-basis = 912 MPa at RTD.

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Spar-cap fiber-direction tensile stress at LC2 ultimate must not exceed Xt_B-basis RTD = 2280 MPa (RTD case). (R2) Same as R1 but under hot/wet knockdown (LC3): stress at LC1 limit must not exceed Xt_HW / 1.5 = 1216 MPa (applying 1.5 ultimate equivalent after knockdown). (R3) Compression stress in spar cap at LC2 must not exceed Xc_B-basis = 1440 MPa RTD. (R4) Tsai-Wu or max-strain failure index in every ply at LC2 ultimate must be <= 1.0. (R5) BVID damage tolerance (LC4): stress at damaged section at ultimate <= 1938 MPa (= 0.85 x Xt_B-basis RTD). (R6) Fatigue at 10^7 cycles: stress amplitude at any spar-cap ply <= 912 MPa. (R7) Blade mass <= 0.9 kg per blade. (R8) Tip deflection at LC1 limit <= 5% of radius = 100 mm.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: composite-shell or continuum-shell FEM with ply-by-ply Tsai-Wu / max-strain evaluation (ANSYS ACP, Abaqus Composites Modeler, Nastran composite). Mesh 10 mm shells along the blade, finer at root attach ligament. Centrifugal force via prescribed rotational velocity load. Fatigue S-N via CMH-17 tabulated curves, hand calc or spreadsheet acceptable. BVID residual-strength with reduced allowable at the damage location is a single additional check. Buckling, modal, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required for this brief; modal forced-response (ground/air resonance) is a real-world concern but out of scope.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of blade structure, mass per blade in kg, spar-cap stress per LC, Tsai-Wu index maximum, BVID residual check, fatigue check at 10^7 cycles, and tip deflection.
short_prompt:
Design a 2.0 m radius IM7/8552 CFRP single-piece rotor blade for a 200 kg-class tail-rotor-style demonstrator at 1200 rpm. Use MIL-HDBK-17 / CMH-17 Vol. 1 B-basis allowables with a 0.80 hot/wet (82 C / wet) knockdown, 0.85 BVID residual-strength factor at the spar cap after a 6 J impact, and the CMH-17 S-N curve for UD tape. Ultimate = 1.5 x limit.

Envelope: 120 mm constant chord, 12% t/c symmetric section (14.4 mm section height); UD IM7/8552 spar caps 80 mm wide x 2-5 mm thick (nominal 3 mm); plain-weave 8552/AS4 web (4-ply +/-45) and skin (2-ply +/-45); two 12 mm Ti root bushings 50 mm apart. Root fitting is out of scope.

Load cases (root).
- LC1 steady flight RTD: axial 12,640 N + flap bending 120 N*m + lag bending 30 N*m.
- LC2 ultimate 1.5 x LC1 RTD: axial 18,960 N + flap 180 N*m.
- LC3 hot/wet degraded LC1: 0.80 knockdown on strengths.
- LC4 BVID residual strength after 6 J mid-span impact: reduced allowable 0.85 x Xt B-basis.
- LC5 fatigue at 10^7 cycles: stress amplitude per CMH-17 S-N (~0.4 x Xt at R = 0.1).

prompt.title: IM7/8552 CFRP rotor blade with CMH-17 B-basis allowables
prompt.brief: 2.0 m radius tail-rotor-style composite blade, 1200 rpm, CMH-17 B-basis Xt/Xc/Yt/Yc/S with 0.80 hot/wet knockdown, BVID residual 0.85, fatigue 10^7.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Xt B-basis RTD per CMH-17 IM7/8552 UD
0.85 x Xt B-basis = damage-tolerance residual strength
CMH-17 S-N ~0.4 x Xt at 10^7 cycles for UD IM7/8552 at R=0.1

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Rotor dynamics (flap/lag/pitch modes, ground resonance) out of scope.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Hot/wet knockdown applied as scalar; no transient thermal.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Rotor caps tension-dominated under centrifugal; not driving.
